perturbation.original_text: 1.1 LICENSE. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, PivX hereby grants to Detto a non-transferable, exclusive license to distribute PivX's Qwik-Fix Pro and any documentation supporting Qwik-Fix Pro provided from time to time by PivX (the "Documentation") within North America, solely to third parties to whom Detto licenses Qwik-Fix Pro ("Third Parties"), and as governed by the terms set forth in Exhibit A (PivX/Detto Reseller Agreement Addendum). PivX also hereby grants to Detto a non-transferable, non-exclusive license to distribute PivX's Qwik-Fix Pro and the Documentation outside of North America, solely to Third Parties and as governed by the terms in Exhibit A. Detto shall have no right to reproduce Qwik-Fix Pro or any part thereof. All copies of Qwik-Fix Pro distributed by Detto shall be distributed pursuant to PivX's current Enterprise License Agreement, as amended by PivX from time to time (the "License"), a copy of which PivX will provide Detto. Detto shall not distribute Qwik-Fix Pro to any Third Party unless the Third Party has accepted the terms of PivX's current License under penalty of perjury and in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 LICENSE. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, PivX hereby grants to Detto a non-transferable, exclusive license to distribute PivX's Qwik-Fix Pro and any documentation supporting Qwik-Fix Pro provided from time to time by PivX (the "Materials") within North America, solely to third parties to whom Detto licenses Qwik-Fix Pro ("Third Parties"), and as governed by the terms set forth in Exhibit A (PivX/Detto Reseller Agreement Addendum). PivX also hereby grants to Detto a non-transferable, non-exclusive license to distribute PivX's Qwik-Fix Pro and the Documentation outside of North America, solely to Third Parties and as governed by the terms in Exhibit A. Detto shall have no right to reproduce Qwik-Fix Pro or any part thereof. All copies of Qwik-Fix Pro distributed by Detto shall be distributed pursuant to PivX's current Enterprise License Agreement, as amended by PivX from time to time (the "License"), a copy of which PivX will provide Detto. Detto shall not distribute Qwik-Fix Pro to any Third Party unless the Third Party has accepted the terms of PivX's current License under penalty of perjury and in writing.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Documentation' to 'Materials' in the definition of the license grant creates confusion. The term 'Documentation' is later used elsewhere in the contract, and it's unclear if 'Materials' is intended to encompass everything that was originally meant by 'Documentation' or if 'Documentation' only refers to documentation outside of North America. This in-text contradiction causes uncertainty about the scope of the license granted and which items are part of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2 OWNERSHIP. As between PivX and Detto, PivX owns and retains all right, title, and interest in and to Qwik-Fix Pro and Documentation; all trademarks, service marks or trade names associated with Qwik-Fix Pro or Documentation (the "Trademarks"); all copyrights, patents, trade secret rights, and other intellectual property rights therein (collectively, together with Qwik-Fix Pro, Documentation, and Trademarks, the "Property"). Except as expressly granted herein, PivX does not grant to Detto any right or license, either express or implied, in Qwik-Fix Pro, Documentation or Property. Detto shall not reverse engineer, disassemble, decompile, or otherwise attempt to derive source code from Qwik-Fix Pro.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Documentation' to 'Materials' in the license grant creates a strong in-text contradiction. Section 1.2 explicitly states PivX owns all right, title, and interest in 'Qwik-Fix Pro and Documentation'. By changing 'Documentation' to 'Materials' in Section 1.1 for the North America license, it becomes unclear whether the license granted to Detto in North America includes the same 'Documentation' that PivX retains ownership of in Section 1.2. This creates ambiguity about the scope of the license and the definition of 'Property,' leading to a direct conflict and uncertainty about Detto's rights.

perturbation.original_text: 2.3 SUPPORT FOR QWIK-FIX PRO. Detto shall provide for all customer support for Qwik-Fix Pro that it resells as governed by the terms set forth in Exhibit A.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3 SUPPORT FOR QWIK-FIX PRO. Detto shall provide for all user assistance for Qwik-Fix Pro that it resells as governed by the terms set forth in Exhibit A.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'customer support' to 'user assistance' introduces ambiguity. While generally synonymous, legal contracts require precision. It is unclear whether Detto must now provide less or more services than originally specified, or whether it includes 'user assistance' instead of 'customer support'. This contradiction arises in section 2.8, where the obligations of Detto to its customers are defined. The definition of support and assistance will affect that enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.8 FULFILLMENT OF DETTO'S OBLIGATIONS. Detto covenants that it will fulfill all of its contractual and legal obligations to its customers. Detto covenants that it will (a) provide its customers with first quality sales and technical support with respect to any copies or licenses of Qwik-Fix Pro sold by Detto, (b) promptly, courteously and appropriately respond to its customers questions, concerns and complaints, and (c) generally deal with its customers in a professional manner that shall add to the good reputation of Detto and PivX. To the extent that Detto fails to fulfill its contractual and legal obligations to its customers, Detto agrees that PivX may elect to fulfill those obligations, or any portion of them, and Detto shall reimburse PivX any costs and expenses so incurred by PivX. Detto agrees to indemnify and hold PivX harmless in accordance with the provisions of Section 4.4 to the extent that it breaches any of its covenants under this Section 2.8.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'customer support' to 'user assistance' introduces ambiguity regarding the scope of Detto's obligations to its customers. Section 2.8 explicitly requires Detto to provide 'first quality sales and technical support' and 'promptly, courteously and appropriately respond to its customers questions, concerns and complaints'. The modified term 'user assistance' lacks the specificity of 'sales and technical support', potentially allowing Detto to provide a lesser standard of care than originally intended and leading to uncertainty about whether Detto is fulfilling its obligations as outlined in section 2.8. This misalignment constitutes a strong in-text contradiction because it creates a conflict in the definition of Detto's obligation.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 PRODUCT WARRANTY. With respect to Qwik-Fix Pro delivered by PivX to Detto on CD-Rom, PivX warrants that for a period of thirty (30) days following delivery to Detto, the media on which Qwik-Fix Pro is furnished to Detto will be free from defects in materials and workmanship during normal use. PivX warrants that Qwik-Fix Pro will substantially conform to the user documentation. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY SET FORTH ABOVE, QWIK-FIX PRO AND DOCUMENTATION ARE PROVIDED "AS IS". PIVX HEREBY DISCLAIMS ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED, OR STATUTORY, INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION, THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 PRODUCT WARRANTY. With respect to Qwik-Fix Pro delivered by PivX to Detto on CD-Rom, PivX warrants that for a period of thirty (30) days following delivery to Detto, the media on which Qwik-Fix Pro is furnished to Detto will be free from defects in materials and workmanship during normal use. PivX warrants that Qwik-Fix Pro will substantially conform to the operating manual. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY SET FORTH ABOVE, QWIK-FIX PRO AND DOCUMENTATION ARE PROVIDED "AS IS". PIVX HEREBY DISCLAIMS ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED, OR STATUTORY, INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION, THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'user documentation' to 'operating manual' within the product warranty creates a subtle contradiction, especially in the context of 'Documentation' being referenced later in the same section. Now the Qwik-Fix Pro must only conform to the 'operating manual' and not necessarily the 'user documentation', creating doubt as to which product literature its expected to conform to. It introduces an internal conflict regarding the scope of the warranty; if the operating manual and user documentation contain different information, which one takes precedence for warranty purposes?
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 PRODUCT WARRANTY. With respect to Qwik-Fix Pro delivered by PivX to Detto on CD-Rom, PivX warrants that for a period of thirty (30) days following delivery to Detto, the media on which Qwik-Fix Pro is furnished to Detto will be free from defects in materials and workmanship during normal use. PivX warrants that Qwik-Fix Pro will substantially conform to the operating manual. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY SET FORTH ABOVE, QWIK-FIX PRO AND DOCUMENTATION ARE PROVIDED "AS IS". PIVX HEREBY DISCLAIMS ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED, OR STATUTORY, INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION, THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'user documentation' to 'operating manual' introduces a contradiction because the original warranty states that Qwik-Fix Pro should conform to the 'user documentation'. However, the modified text indicates that it should conform to the 'operating manual'. The later reference to 'Documentation' in the disclaimer 'QWIK-FIX PRO AND DOCUMENTATION ARE PROVIDED "AS IS"' creates ambiguity as it is unclear if this refers to both or only one. If the operating manual and user documentation contain different specifications, then PivX's obligations become unclear, causing a strong in-text contradiction. The contradiction exists because, in the changed version, the warranty compliance hinges on the 'operating manual' only, whereas the documentation disclaimer could be interpreted as referring to a broader set of documents, potentially in contradiction.
